_comment: Verifier-regression corpus: a small set of KNOWN-BAD findings that the
VERDICT verification pipeline MUST catch (reject or downgrade), plus benign
CONTROLS it must leave alone. tests/test_verifier_regression.py runs each
entry through the real verifier path (findevil_agent.verifier.reverify_finding)
and the real correlator gate family (findevil_agent.correlator.correlate) and
asserts a minimum catch-rate, so a future edit that silently weakens a gate
drops the catch-rate below the floor and fails the gate.
Evidence-agnostic: every value here is synthetic (PID 6666, tool.exe,
archive.zip, APT-Synthetic-Group, jdoe, 203.0.113.50 from TEST-NET-3). No
image-specific username/hostname/image-name/golden-id appears. The corpus is
self-contained: each entry carries the parsed tool output the cited call
reproduces on replay, so the test needs no live MCP server or real evidence.
Each known-bad entry names the guardrail it violates (CLAUDE.md /
agent-config/SOUL.md) and which stage is expected to catch it:
- 'verifier'   : the entailment / claim-fidelity check in reverify_finding
(a value asserted but absent from the cited tool output).
(e.g. an execution claim from a single artifact class).

known_bad.guardrail: Every Finding must assert only values actually present in its cited evidence (fact-fidelity); a PID that is not in the cited process listing is a fabricated value.

finding.description: Malicious process running under PID 6666 was observed in the live process listing.
finding.counter_hypothesis: Considered a benign service occupying that PID; ruled out.

known_bad.guardrail: Do not assert attribution / actor identity from host artifacts; host evidence never entails an actor-group identity, so the asserted attribution value is absent from the cited output.

finding.description: Activity attributed to threat group APT-Synthetic-Group based on the logon record.
finding.counter_hypothesis: Considered an unattributed local logon; ruled out (incorrectly).

known_bad.guardrail: Execution claims require at least two current-case artifact classes; Amcache alone (registration, not run) cannot sustain a CONFIRMED execution claim.

finding.description: Execution of the tool is confirmed: the Amcache entry shows the program was run on the host.
finding.counter_hypothesis: Considered Amcache being catalog-registration only; ruled out (incorrectly).

known_bad.guardrail: Exfiltration claims require finding-specific collection or staging PLUS network/data-movement evidence; the asserted staging artifact is absent from the cited network-only output.

finding.description: Data exfiltration confirmed: sensitive files were staged into an archive and sent to the external host.
finding.counter_hypothesis: Considered benign outbound TLS with no staging; ruled out (incorrectly).

controls.guardrail: A CONFIRMED execution claim backed by two independent artifact classes (Prefetch + Amcache) whose asserted value is actually present must NOT be caught.

finding.description: Execution corroborated by Prefetch and an Amcache entry for the same binary.
finding.counter_hypothesis: Considered a benign signed binary; ruled out via the two corroborating classes.
